channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Punjab Government Expands Hypertension Screening, Treatment and Prevention Programmes
item.description: On World Hypertension Day, Punjab’s healthcare initiatives are taking significant strides in combating high blood pressure, commonly known as the 'silent killer'. The Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojna (MMSY) is...

item.title: Rick Steves Reflects on His First Solo European Trip in 1973
item.description: In 1973, Rick Steves embarked on a transformative journey that would shape his future career and lifelong passion for travel. Freshly graduated from Edmonds High School in Washington, Steves left for ...

item.title: Eskom achieves a full year of uninterrupted electricity supply, marking grid stability
item.description: Eskom has marked a significant milestone by delivering a full year without any load-shedding, the first time since 2018. This achievement reflects a major improvement in South Africa's national electr...

item.title: Mohammed Al-Makura Becomes Sole APC Candidate for Lafia/Obi Federal Constituency
item.description: Mohammed Al-Makura don secure di All Progressives Congress (APC) ticket for Lafia/Obi Federal Constituency after Hon. Isaac Ali Kigbu decide to withdraw from di race. Al-Makura wey don serve before as...

item.title: The Value of Letting Go: How Flexibility in Beliefs Shapes Personal Growth
item.description: In this reflective piece, Nadine Levy explores the dynamic role beliefs play in our lives. She recounts a conference conversation where rigid attachment to ideas transformed dialogue into a one-sided ...

item.title: La Salle Lady Spikers set sights on a perfect 32-0 season, says Shevana Laput
item.description: De La Salle University’s Lady Spikers are aiming for a historic feat in UAAP Season 88 as they set their sights on achieving a perfect 32-0 record. Team captain Shevana Laput expressed that while the ...

item.title: Bruno Fernandes sets assist record as Casemiro bids farewell at Manchester United
item.description: Manchester United secured a 3-2 victory over Nottingham Forest in a game marked by VAR controversy and emotional farewells. Casemiro received a standing ovation as he left Old Trafford for the last ti...

item.title: A Glimpse of 19th-Century Cornwall Through Photochrom Prints
item.description: In the late 19th century, Cornwall was a region of striking contrasts, balancing its industrial past with the burgeoning tourism brought by the arrival of the Great Western Railway. Once dominated by ...

item.title: DGIP Plans Nationwide Home Delivery of Passports and Digital Services
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Directorate General of Immigration and Passports (DGIP) is going to launch a convenient new service for Pakistani citizens. According to Passports DG Muhammad Ali Randhawa, applicants w...

item.title: Early detection and prevention crucial in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
item.description: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, commonly known as COPD, is a leading cause of death worldwide, accounting for 3.5 million deaths in 2021 alone. Many people believe it is only a disease affectin...

item.title: Review: Conrad Black's Multi-Volume History of the World
item.description: In this article, Father Raymond J. de Souza praises Conrad Black’s extensive work as a historian, highlighting his multi-volume project, 'The Political and Strategic History of the World.' Black, who ...

item.title: Keanu Cupido Returns to Strengthen Sundowns Defence for CAF Final First Leg
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns have received a significant boost ahead of their CAF Champions League final first leg against AS FAR at Loftus Versfeld, with coach Miguel Cardoso confirming the inclusion of defende...

item.title: Yusuf Gagdi Loses APC House of Reps Ticket to John Tonshinen in Plateau
item.description: For Plateau State, na big political wahala don show after the APC House of Representatives primaries wey happen across Nigeria on Saturday. The current lawmaker, Yusuf Gagdi wey dey represent Pankshin...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rain and Strong Winds in Several Counties
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather alert indicating that certain regions across the country will experience rainfall with increased intensity over the next 24 hours. Areas ...

item.title: Sokoto State Approves ₦1.46 Billion for Official Vehicles Amid Economic and Security Challenges
item.description: The Sokoto State Government don approve the sum of ₦1.46 billion to buy official vehicles for Governor Ahmed Aliyu’s office, which go include bulletproof SUVs, Prado jeeps, and a Coaster bus. This dec...

item.title: Imo APC inaugurates appeal committee, opens window for House of Representatives primary election complaints
item.description: The Imo State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC) has officially opened a petition window for aspirants who get grievances from the just concluded House of Representatives primary elections...

item.title: RSU Management Dismisses Allegations Against Lecturer as Witch-Hunt
item.description: According to Rivers State University management, the recent report wey dem raise against one of their lecturers, Dr. Dike Harcourt Whyte, na pure witch-hunt aimed at spoiling the man image and the uni...

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency, Sparking Political Reactions
item.description: The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. The announcement triggered widespre...

item.title: Bulgarian Singer DARA Wins Eurovision 2026 with Hit Song Bangaranga
item.description: DARA, born Darina Yotova in Varna, Bulgaria, has become the first Bulgarian artist to win the Eurovision Song Contest, taking the crown in 2026 with her vibrant and infectious track Bangaranga. At 27,...

item.title: Oburu Oginga Urges Party Discipline and Affirms ODM’s Role Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: On Sunday, May 17, 2026, Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) elder statesman Oburu Oginga addressed concerns about internal party discipline and leadership, particularly responding to criticism from Lind...
